name: pipeline
description: Design, run, and self-improve long-chain AI workflows as ordered, self-contained iteration files under the consumer project's .claude/pipeline/. A deterministic Bun CLI (`pipeline next`) owns all orchestration control flow; a thin pipeline-manager subagent executes each step in a fresh-context step-executor, and a two-tier self-improvement loop (between-step improver + end-of-run retrospective) feeds execution-discovered knowledge back into the pipeline's own docs. /pipeline:dispatch matches tasks to pipelines through a three-tier cost ladder (deterministic BM25 → Haiku disambiguator → chain detection) so most matches cost near-zero LLM tokens. Every run is measured by pure software (no LLM) into reviewable text files under .claude/pipeline/.stats/ — durations, per-step timings, outcomes, transcript-folded tokens, and TOOL FAILURES (per-tool fail counts in each run record + per-failure detail lines with step attribution in the run log; headless runs fold their pinned per-step session transcripts for exact attribution) (on by default; PIPELINE_STATS_ENABLED=0 disables) — with a user-invoked-only /pipeline:optimize skill that turns the measurements, including recurring tool-failure patterns, into targeted pipeline improvements (pipeline-attributable failures get fixes; environment noise is only reported). Optional: parallel/DAG execution in git worktrees, conditional routing graphs, consumer-provisioned external worktrees, per-step model AND reasoning-effort pinning via frontmatter (plus per-run --model/--effort step overrides at invocation; effort levels low|medium|high|xhigh|max, inherit by default, applied for real by the headless runner via claude --effort and by dashboard chat via the Agent SDK), an interactive headless runner (`pipeline drive`: schema-validated step records, pinned sessions with needs-input question/answer resume, crash-resume, per-step permission modes, --task delivery), and an opt-in live dashboard (off by default via PIPELINE_UI_ENABLED) that is mobile-responsive and can launch runs (searchable pipeline picker; typed or DICTATED task — auto-detected language incl. mixed ru+en; per-step model overrides), answer a parked run's questions, STOP/cancel any active or stale run, show per-run analytics with live elapsed + per-step wall-clock timings (the TOOLS/FAIL/AGENTS tiles each open a drill-down: per-tool call durations and aggregates, per-failure tool+input+error detail, and per-agent duration+token spend from each agent's own transcript), render target-family runs as their full chain (target entry steps + the hub's shared steps, with per-step configured model/effort/permission chips and full token breakdowns that refresh on any file edit), switch between live runs, edit pipeline files in the browser (with an AI-Fix button that repairs validate-lint issues via a background claude -p session), and self-update: when a newer plugin version is installed while the daemon runs an older one, the top bar grows an Update & Restart button (also `pipeline ui --restart`) that hands the daemon off to the new version on the same port with open tabs reloading themselves.
